blocks:
Ayahs

عَمَّ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (۱)
What is it that they are questioning each other about?! (1)
عَنِ ٱلنَّبَإِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۲)
[Is it] about the Great Tiding, (2)
ٱلَّذِى هُمْ فِيهِ مُخْتَلِفُونَ (۳)
the one about which they differ? (3)
كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ (٤)
No indeed! Soon they will know! (4)
ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ (٥)
No indeed! Soon they will know for once again! (5)

Key Points

The word naba' (نبأ) means an important and certain piece of news, and it is understood from the subsequent verses that what is meant by it is the news of the establishment of the Resurrection (Qiyamah). In narrations, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام has been introduced as one of the manifestations (masadiq) of the "Great News" (Naba' Adhim).(1) Regarding the Resurrection (Ma'ad), the disbelievers fall into several groups: «الذى هم فیه مختلفون» (in which they are differing). Some consider it impossible, some consider it remote, some harbor doubt, and others show obstinacy. Questioning is sometimes for the purpose of understanding a matter, which is affirmed and emphasized by the Quran, as He says: «فسئلوا اهل الذكر»(2) But sometimes questioning is intended to create skepticism and doubt in the minds of others, especially regarding certain and definite matters like the occurrence of the Resurrection, which the Quran condemns in these verses.

1) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 2) An-Nahl, 43.

Messages

1- Beginning a discourse by posing a question is effective in influencing the speech. «عمّ یتساءلون»

2- Casting doubt on the Resurrection is the action of the disbelievers. «یتساءلون عن النبأ العظیم»

3- If the question is natural, an answer must be given: «یسئلونك ... قل» (They ask you... Say). But if it is mischief, a confrontation is necessary. «كلا سیعلمون»

4- In the face of mockery and insinuation against sacred matters and definite beliefs, one must speak with decisiveness. «كلاّ»

5- In the face of doubt, the truth must be repeated. «كلاّ سیعلمون...كلاّ سیعلمون»

6- The Resurrection is not far off. «سیعلمون» (The letter sin [س] is a sign of proximity/nearness).

7- The Resurrection is the Day of the uncovering of realities. «سیعلمون»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah An-Naba'

This Surah has forty verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is derived from the second verse, which refers to the Resurrection as the "Great News" (Naba' Adhim). The verses of this Surah, by arguing based on the wise system governing nature, consider the establishment of the Resurrection—for the implementation of the system of reward and punishment—to be a necessity of Divine Wisdom, and deem human existence without it to be futile and vain. The continuation of the Surah describes a part of the severe punishment of the transgressors and the blessings of the people of Paradise, and concludes with a strong warning to the disbelievers.

Ayahs

أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِهَـٰدًا (٦)
Did We not make the earth a resting place? (6)
وَٱلْجِبَالَ أَوْتَادًا (۷)
and the mountains stakes? (7)
وَخَلَقْنَـٰكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا (۸)
and create you in pairs? (8)
وَجَعَلْنَا نَوْمَكُمْ سُبَاتًا (۹)
and make your sleep for rest? (9)
وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلَّيْلَ لِبَاسًا (۱٠)
and make the night a covering? (10)
وَجَعَلْنَا ٱلنَّهَارَ مَعَاشًا (۱۱)
and make the day for livelihood? (11)

Key Points

Awtaad (أوتاد) is the plural of watad (وتد), meaning "peg" or "stake." The comparison of mountains to pegs is one of the scientific miracles of the Quran. The roots of the mountains deep within the earth prevent the movement of the earth's layers and the occurrence of constant earthquakes. Mountains, like pegs, are embedded in the earth several times deeper than what is visible on the surface. Furthermore, mountains have other benefits: they preserve snow for the summer, block strong winds, serve as landmarks and signs for roads, are the location of minerals, stones, and caves, and are the cause of the creation of valleys, each of which is essential for life in its own right. Subaat (سُبات) means the cessation and suspension of work, which leads to the comfort and tranquility of the body and soul.

Messages

1- A handful is a sample of the whole (a drop in the ocean). Studying existence is the best way to know Allah and to know the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «ألَم نَجْعَل الارْضَ مهاداً...»

2- In the court of reason and conscience, man is a worshipper of God, and one can question his conscience regarding Divine power. «ألَم نَجْعَل»

3- Use the power of Allah in the creation of the earth, the heavens, and other natural phenomena as an argument for His power to establish the Day of Judgment. «الارْضَ... الجبال... الّیل... النّهار...»

4- After rejecting false ideas, argue for your true beliefs. «كلاّ سیعلمون... ألَم نَجْعَل»

5- If there is no spirit of obstinacy, man can learn lessons from this very earth, time, sleep, and sustenance. «الارْضَ... الّیل... النّهار...»

6- The Divine power and wisdom in the creation of us and the universe is proof that He will not abandon us with death. «خلقناكم ازواجاً... نومكم سباتاً... النّهار معاشاً»

Ayahs

وَبَنَيْنَا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعًا شِدَادًا (۱۲)
and build above you the seven mighty heavens? (12)
وَجَعَلْنَا سِرَاجًا وَهَّاجًا (۱۳)
and make [the sun for] a radiant lamp? (13)
وَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلْمُعْصِرَٰتِ مَآءً ثَجَّاجًا (۱٤)
and send down water pouring from the rain-clouds, (14)
لِّنُخْرِجَ بِهِۦ حَبًّا وَنَبَاتًا (۱٥)
that We may bring forth with it grains and plants, (15)
وَجَنَّـٰتٍ أَلْفَافًا (۱٦)
and luxuriant gardens? (16)

Key Points

The term Mu'sirāt (معصرات) comes from Asr (عصر), meaning squeezing. It is either an attribute of rain-bearing clouds, which seem to squeeze themselves to release rain, or it is an attribute of the winds that make the clouds dense and compressed so that rain falls from them. (3)

(3) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Allah has established the administration of existence upon a specific system (the system of causality and cause and effect). (The light and heat of the sun, along with clouds and rain, become the factor for producing grain and vegetation.) «سراجاً وهّاجاً... ماءً سجاجاً لنخرج به حبّاً و نباتاً»

2- The Qur'an is not poetry, but it possesses a special rhythm and music. «وهّاجاً، ثجّاجاً، نباتاً»

3- Natural means and instruments should not make us heedless of Allah. «لنخرج به...»

4- The God who transforms the dead earth into a lush garden full of trees through the descent of rain is not incapable of resurrecting the dead. «لنخرج به... جنّات الفافاً»

Ayahs

إِنَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْفَصْلِ كَانَ مِيقَـٰتًا (۱۷)
Indeed the Day of Judgement is the tryst, (17)
يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْوَاجًا (۱۸)
the day the Trumpet will be blown, and you will come in groups, (18)
وَفُتِحَتِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ فَكَانَتْ أَبْوَٰبًا (۱۹)
and the sky will be opened and become gates, (19)
وَسُيِّرَتِ ٱلْجِبَالُ فَكَانَتْ سَرَابًا (۲٠)
and the mountains will be set moving and become a mirage. (20)

Key Points

The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is called "Yawm al-Jam'" (the Day of Gathering) because everyone will assemble therein, and it is also called "Yawm al-Fasl" (the Day of Separation/Decision) because the righteous will be separated from the wicked, the truth from falsehood, and disputes will be settled. We read in narrations that on the Day of Judgment, people will be divided into various groups, and each group will appear in a specific form: the slanderer in the guise of a monkey, the consumer of unlawful earnings (haram) in the form of a pig, the usurer (riba-eater) in an inverted form, the oppressive judge as blind, the arrogant as deaf and mute, the scholar without action chewing his tongue, the neighbor-harasser without hands or feet, the tale-bearer hung over fire, the lustful person with a foul odor, and those who boast about their clothing will be resurrected wearing the garments of Hell. (4) The movement of the mountains is the prelude to their collision, the crushing and pulverization of the stones, and finally, their flowing away in such a manner that they appear like a mirage from afar. "و سُیّرت الجبال فكانت سراباً" (And the mountains shall be set in motion, and they shall become a mirage.)

4) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- This world is the prelude to the Day of Judgment, and all these blessings are not futile or vain. "انّ یَوم الْفصل كانَ میقاتاً"

2- The time of the Day of Judgment is known to Allah beforehand. "كان میقاتاً"

3- The Day of Judgment also has an order/system. "یَوم یُنفخُ فى الصُّور"

4- On the Day of Judgment, the order of the heavens and the earth will be transformed. "فتحت السماء...سیّرت الجبال"

5- Man is not abandoned or forgotten upon death. "یوم الفصل كان میقاتاً"

6- Do not be overly attached to worldly ties and relationships, for one day everything will be severed. "یوم الفصل"

7- The connection between the earth and the heaven, and their inhabitants, will be expanded on the Day of Judgment. "فتحت السماء"

Ayahs

إِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ كَانَتْ مِرْصَادًا (۲۱)
Indeed hell is in ambush, (21)
لِّلطَّـٰغِينَ مَـَٔابًا (۲۲)
a resort for the rebels, (22)
لَّـٰبِثِينَ فِيهَآ أَحْقَابًا (۲۳)
to reside therein for ages, (23)
لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَرْدًا وَلَا شَرَابًا (۲٤)
tasting in it neither any coolness nor drink, (24)
إِلَّا حَمِيمًا وَغَسَّاقًا (۲٥)
except boiling water and pus, (25)
جَزَآءً وِفَاقًا (۲٦)
a fitting requital. (26)
إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَرْجُونَ حِسَابًا (۲۷)
Indeed they did not expect any reckoning, (27)
وَكَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا كِذَّابًا (۲۸)
and they denied Our signs mendaciously, (28)
وَكُلَّ شَىْءٍ أَحْصَيْنَـٰهُ كِتَـٰبًا (۲۹)
and We have figured everything in a Book. (29)
فَذُوقُوا۟ فَلَن نَّزِيدَكُمْ إِلَّا عَذَابًا (۳٠)
So [now] taste! We shall increase you in nothing but punishment! (30)

Key Points

Marṣād means an ambush site (or lurking place), and ṭāghīn, derived from ṭughyān, means going excessively far in disobedience. Ma'āb, derived from awb, means a dwelling place and return. The ultimate outcome (the end) is called ma'āb because man prepares it through his own deeds and now returns to it. Aḥqāb is the plural of ḥuqb, meaning a long duration. We read in narrations: Aḥqāb is the plural of ḥuqb, and each ḥuqb is sixty years, and according to some, eighty years, where each day of it is like a thousand years that you count in this world. (5) Ḥamīm is boiling water, and ghassāq is the yellowish fluid that oozes from wounds, pus, and blood. The meaning of the Ayah might be that Hell is lying in wait for everyone, but the pious (muttaqīn) will pass over it, and it will become the dwelling place for the criminals. Regarding "للطّاغین مئابا" (a return for the transgressors), considering Divine Justice, where punishment and recompense correspond to beliefs and deeds, "جزاءً وفاقاً" (a fitting recompense), the increase in torment mentioned in the Ayah "فلن نَزیدكم الاّ عذاباً" (We shall not increase you except in torment) is due to the increase in disbelief and fleeing from the truth. "فلم یزدهم دعائى الاّ فرارا" (My call only increased their flight) (6). Since the flight of the disbelievers increases in response to every call from the prophets, Allah likewise increases their torment. In Hell, instead of coolness, there is heat, and instead of drink, there is ghassāq. "برداً و لا شراباً الاّ حمیماً و غسّاقاً" (neither coolness nor drink, except boiling water and ghassāq).

5) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 6) Nuh, 6.

Messages

1- Hell is lying in wait for the transgressors even now. "انّ جهنّم كانت مرصاداً"

2- The capture and entrapment of criminals is a certainty. "مرصاداً للطّاغین"

3- Transgression (ṭughyān) is the sign of the inhabitants of Hell. "للطّاغین"

4- Divine punishments are just. "جزاءً وفاقاً"

5- Even the possibility of accountability and reckoning on the Day of Judgment is sufficient to control man. "لایرجون حسابا"

6- Lack of belief in the Day of Judgment is the key to transgression. "لطّاغین... لایرجون حساباً"

7- Denying the Day of Judgment paves the way for denying everything. "لا یرجون حسابا و كذبوا بآیاتنا كذّابا"

8- All deeds, minor and major, are subject to reckoning, and Allah has comprehensive knowledge of everything, and this comprehensiveness is fixed and permanent. (Kitāban is the symbol of fixity and permanence.) "كلّ شى ء احصیناه كتاباً"

Ayahs

إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ مَفَازًا (۳۱)
Indeed a triumph awaits the Godwary: (31)
حَدَآئِقَ وَأَعْنَـٰبًا (۳۲)
gardens and vineyards, (32)
وَكَوَاعِبَ أَتْرَابًا (۳۳)
and buxom maidens of a like age, (33)
وَكَأْسًا دِهَاقًا (۳٤)
and brimming cups. (34)
لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا وَلَا كِذَّٰبًا (۳٥)
Therein they shall hear neither vain talk nor lies (35)
جَزَآءً مِّن رَّبِّكَ عَطَآءً حِسَابًا (۳٦)
—a reward and a sufficing bounty from your Lord, (36)

Key Points

Mafāz (مفاز) either means the place of attainment (success/felicity) or attainment itself. Hadā'iq (حدائق) is the plural of Hadīqah (حدیقة), which refers to a walled garden. Kawā'ib (كواعب) is the plural of Kā'ib (كاعب), referring to a girl whose breasts have just begun to swell (a young maiden). Atrāb (اتراب) is the plural of Tarb (تَرب), meaning peers in age, and similar in form and appearance. Perhaps the intention is that the Houris of Paradise do not surpass one another in beauty. The word kās (كاس) means a crystal cup, and dihāq (دهاق) means overflowing (brimming). Of course, some derive it from the root dahaqa (دَهَقَ), meaning to tie repeatedly, implying that cups of wine are served continuously. Grapes hold a special place among fruits, and thus they are mentioned separately: «حدائق و اعناباً» (gardens and grapes).

Messages

1- Fear and hope must exist side by side. In this Surah, the reward for the righteous (muttaqīn) is placed opposite the punishment for the transgressors (ṭāghīn). «انّ جهنّم كانَت مرصاداً للطاغین...انّ لِلمتقین مفازا»

2- Attainment and felicity are achieved through material and spiritual blessings. «انّ لِلمتقین مفازا» (The word hadā'iq (حدائق) symbolizes material blessings, and the word lā yasma'ūn (لایسمعون) symbolizes spiritual blessings.)

3- The blessings of Paradise do not cause a person to fall into arrogance, heedlessness, vain talk, or intoxication. «لایسمعون»

4- Gatherings in which there is no vanity (laghw) or falsehood are heavenly gatherings. «لایسمعون فیها لغواً و لا كذّاباً»

5- Not only are the punishments just, «جزاءً وفاقاً», but the rewards are also calculated and measured. «عطاءً حساباً»

6- In the heavenly society, individuals do not accuse one another of lying, and trust and confidence prevail among them. «و لا كذّاباً»

7- Divine rewards are His gift and grace, not something we have a claim to. «جزاءً من ربّك عطاءً»

8- The requirement of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) is a system of punishment and reward. «جزاء من ربّك»

9- The Divine reward for the righteous (muttaqīn) is calculated. Whoever possesses greater piety (taqwā) will have a greater reward. «عطاءً حساباً»

Ayah

رَّبِّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ۖ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ مِنْهُ خِطَابًا (۳۷)
the All-beneficent, the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. They will not be able to address Him (37)

Messages

1- The administration of the affairs of all existence is uniform for Allah. The heavens, the earth, and what is between them make no difference to Him. «ربّ السموات و الارض و مابینهما»

2- The Noble Prophet holds a very high status. It is as if he is on one side of the scale and all existence is on the other. «ربّك... ربّ السموات و الارض»

3- Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) is based on mercy. «ربّ - الرحمن»

4- There is no right to arbitrary or protesting conversation on the Day of Resurrection. «لا یملكون منه خطاباً»

Ayahs

يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلرُّوحُ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ صَفًّا ۖ لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا (۳۸)
on the day when the Spirit and the angels stand in an array: none shall speak except someone who is permitted by the All-beneficent and says what is rightful. (38)
ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ فَمَن شَآءَ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ مَـَٔابًا (۳۹)
That day is true for certain. So let anyone who wishes take resort with his Lord. (39)

Key Points

The word "Rūḥ" (Spirit) sometimes appears in the Qur'an in an absolute form, such as in this Ayah and Ayah 4 of Sūrat Al-Qadr, "تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ" (The angels and the Spirit descend), where it is usually placed opposite the angels (al-Malā'ikah). And sometimes it appears in a restricted form, such as Rūḥ al-Qudus (the Holy Spirit) and Rūḥ al-Amīn (the Trustworthy Spirit). It is understood from narrations that the Spirit is either a creature superior to the angel, or if it is an angel, it is the most superior of the angels. As we read in a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: The Spirit is an angel superior to Gabriel and Michael. (7) In narrations, we read that the intent of "إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمَٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا" (except him whom the Most Gracious permits and who speaks rightly) are the infallible Imams, who are permitted by Allah to speak what is right and true. (8)

7) Tafsir Namūneh. 8) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Not only humans and jinn, but also the angels will be present on the scene of the Resurrection. "يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الرُّوحُ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ"

2- Observing order, discipline, and readiness to execute commands are among the characteristics of the Divine angels. "يَقُومُ الرُّوحُ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ صَفًّا"

3- On the Day of Resurrection, intercession is conditional upon the permission of Allah. "لَا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمَٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا"

4- Man is free in choosing his path. "فَمَن شَاءَ"

5- The Resurrection is a Day whose occurrence is true (Haqq) and on which actions are carried out based on truth. "الْيَوْمُ الْحَقُّ"

6- Since the Resurrection is true (Haqq), let us choose a path that leads to Him. "فَمَن شَاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ مَآبًا"

Ayah

إِنَّآ أَنذَرْنَـٰكُمْ عَذَابًا قَرِيبًا يَوْمَ يَنظُرُ ٱلْمَرْءُ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ وَيَقُولُ ٱلْكَافِرُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى كُنتُ تُرَٰبًۢا (٤٠)
Indeed We have warned you of a punishment near at hand—the day when a person will observe what his hands have sent ahead and the faithless one will say, ‘I wish I were dust!’ (40)

Messages

1- For the heedless, warning and admonition are more important than glad tidings. «انّا انذرناكم»

2- The reason for heedlessness is that people consider the Day of Resurrection to be distant. «عذاباً قریباً»

3- Regret is proof of man's free will. «یا لیتنى كنت تراباً»

4- On the Day of Resurrection, man will observe his own performance. «ینظر المرء ما قدّمت یداه»

5- The fate of every person in the Hereafter is in his own hands. «ینظر المرء ما قدّمت یداه»

6- Soil takes one seed and yields one cluster (of grain), but the disbelievers hear hundreds of proofs and arguments yet do not accept a single one. Therefore, soil is superior to the disbeliever. «كنت تراباً»
